file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object class for HP Smart Windows app Shortcuts feature automation. Contains the Shortcuts class which inherits from HPSmartBasicActions and provides all Appium tuple locators for shortcuts creation, editing, deletion, and execution workflows including print shortcuts, email shortcuts, cloud storage shortcuts (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox), and scan-to-destination shortcuts with full validation of shortcut lifecycle operations.

classes.class_purpose: Page object class for automating the HP Smart Windows app Shortcuts feature using Appium WinAppDriver. Inherits from HPSmartBasicActions to access base Appium interaction methods. Defines class-level Appium tuple locators for all shortcuts UI elements including shortcut creation wizard controls, toggle switches for print/email/cloud destinations, email input fields, cloud provider sign-in buttons, scan controls, and shortcut management options. Provides complete methods for creating shortcuts with various destination combinations, editing existing shortcuts, deleting shortcuts, executing shortcuts with scan jobs, and validating shortcut completion status.

parameters.name: driver
parameters.description: Appium WebDriver instance from appium_driver fixture for HP Smart Windows app automation

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the Shortcuts page object by calling the parent HPSmartBasicActions constructor with super().__init__(driver) and storing the driver reference as self.driver. All locators are defined as class-level attributes so no locator initialization happens in __init__. This constructor requires an active Appium WinAppDriver session passed from the appium_driver pytest fixture.

Refreshes the current HP Smart app page by calling self.driver.refresh() and then waits 5 seconds using time.sleep(5) for the page to reload completely. This is used to reset the UI state or reload data after operations.

Navigates to the Shortcuts feature page in HP Smart app. First waits for the printer_online status using self.wait_for_object(self.printer_online, self.basic_wait_time) to ensure the printer is ready. Then checks if the shortcuts navigation button is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.shortcuts, self.basic_wait_time). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.shortcuts). If not displayed, logs an error and asserts False to fail the test.

Deletes all existing shortcuts from the Shortcuts list to ensure a clean state before test execution. Loops up to 20 times checking if add_shortcut_dots contextual menu button is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.add_shortcut_dots, timeout=20). If found, clicks it, then checks for the delete option using self.element_displayed(self.delete, timeout=10) and clicks it if present. Calls self.shortcut_delete() to confirm deletion. Logs success and breaks the loop when no more shortcuts are found.

Navigates to the Shortcuts page and ensures all existing shortcuts are deleted before proceeding. Calls self.navigate_to_shortcuts() to open the Shortcuts page, then calls self.delete_existing_shortcut() to remove all existing shortcuts. Finally waits for the add_shortcut button to appear using self.wait_for_object(self.add_shortcut, self.time_10) confirming the page is ready for new shortcut creation.

Clicks the back button in the shortcut creation wizard to return to the Shortcuts list. Checks if back_btn_shortcut is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.back_btn_shortcut, timeout=40). If found, clicks it using self.click_me(self.back_btn_shortcut). Then verifies navigation succeeded by checking if shortcuts button is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.shortcuts, timeout=40). Logs success or failure and asserts False if either check fails.

Navigates to the Create your own Shortcut wizard. Calls self.navigate_save_time_with_shortcuts() to reach the Shortcuts page with clean state. Checks if add_shortcut button is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.add_shortcut, timeout=20) and clicks it using self.click_me(self.add_shortcut). Then waits for the create_your_own_shortcut option to appear using self.wait_for_object(self.create_your_own_shortcut, self.basic_wait_time) confirming the Add Shortcut wizard opened.

Navigates to the shortcut destination configuration screen. Calls self.navigate_to_create_your_own_shortcut() to open the Add Shortcut wizard. Checks if create_your_own_shortcut option is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.create_your_own_shortcut) and clicks it using self.click_me(self.create_your_own_shortcut). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Finally waits for short_cut_name field to appear using self.wait_for_object(self.short_cut_name, timeout=20) confirming the destination configuration screen loaded.

parameters.name: shortcut_name
parameters.description: Name to assign to the shortcut being created

functions.what_it_does: Creates a shortcut with all three destination toggles enabled (print, email, cloud). Clicks create_your_own_shortcut using self.click_element(self.create_your_own_shortcut, self.basic_wait_time). Clicks short_cut_name field using self.click_me(self.short_cut_name), waits 1 second, then types the shortcut_name using pyautogui.write(shortcut_name, interval=0.1). Enables all three toggles by clicking print_toggle, email_toggle, and cloud_toggle using self.click_element() for each.

Verifies the cancel confirmation dialog appears when canceling shortcut creation before saving. Checks if cancel button is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.cancel, timeout=30). If found, clicks it using self.click_me(self.cancel) and logs success. If not found, raises Exception. Then checks if both cancel_confirmation and cancel_decline buttons are displayed. If both present, logs success and clicks cancel_confirmation using self.click_element(self.cancel_confirmation) to confirm cancellation. If not both present, raises Exception indicating incorrect cancel screen.

parameters.name: shortcut_name
parameters.description: Name to assign to the print shortcut

functions.what_it_does: Adds a shortcut with only the print destination enabled. Calls self.navigate_destination_add_shortcut() to reach the destination configuration screen. Clicks short_cut_name field using self.click_me(self.short_cut_name), waits 1 second, then types the shortcut_name using pyautogui.write(shortcut_name, interval=0.1). Checks if short_cut_name is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.short_cut_name, timeout=20). If displayed, clicks print_toggle using self.click_me(self.print_toggle) to enable print destination. If not displayed, asserts False with error message.

parameters.name: shortcut_name
parameters.description: Name for the print shortcut to save

functions.what_it_does: Saves a print-only shortcut after configuration. Calls self.add_shortcut_default_print(shortcut_name) to configure the shortcut. Checks if save_shortcut_name button is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.save_shortcut_name, timeout=15). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.save_shortcut_name) to save the shortcut. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Finally waits for my_shortcuts to appear using self.wait_for_object(self.my_shortcuts, timeout=30) confirming the shortcut was saved and the list view loaded.

Creates and saves a print-only shortcut and verifies it appears in My Shortcuts list. Calls self.save_shortcut_default_print(shortcut_name) to create and save the shortcut. Checks if my_shortcuts is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.my_shortcuts, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.my_shortcuts). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Then checks if add_shortcut_dots is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.add_shortcut_dots, timeout=40). If displayed, logs success message. If not displayed, logs error message indicating creation failed.

Navigates to the edit screen for an existing shortcut. Calls self.navigate_to_shortcuts() to open the Shortcuts page. Checks if add_shortcut is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.add_shortcut, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks add_shortcut_dots contextual menu using self.click_me(self.add_shortcut_dots). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Then checks if edit option is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.edit, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.edit) to open the edit screen. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address to add as recipient in the shortcut

functions.what_it_does: Adds email destination configuration to a shortcut. Checks if email_drop_down is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.email_drop_down, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.email_drop_down) to expand email section. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Checks if email_to field is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.email_to, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.email_to) and types the email address using pyautogui.write(email, interval=0.1). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Clicks email_toggle to enable email destination. Clicks save_shortcut_name to save the changes. Each step includes error checking and assertion.

Navigates to My Shortcuts list and verifies shortcuts are present. Checks if my_shortcuts is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.my_shortcuts, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.my_shortcuts). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Then checks if add_shortcut_dots is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.add_shortcut_dots, timeout=40). If displayed, logs success message indicating shortcuts exist. If not displayed, logs error message and asserts False.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address to add or update in the shortcut

functions.what_it_does: Edits an existing shortcut to add or update email destination. Calls self.navigate_to_edit_shortcut() to open the edit screen. Calls self.add_shortcut_email(email) to configure the email destination. Calls self.my_shortcut() to verify the changes were saved and return to My Shortcuts list.

Initiates deletion of an existing shortcut by opening the delete option. Calls self.navigate_to_shortcuts() to open the Shortcuts page. Checks if add_shortcut is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.add_shortcut, timeout=30). If displayed, clicks add_shortcut_dots contextual menu using self.click_me(self.add_shortcut_dots). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Checks if delete option is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.delete, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.delete). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Does not confirm deletion - that is handled by shortcut_delete() method.

Confirms shortcut deletion in the delete confirmation dialog. Waits for delete_shortcut confirmation dialog to appear using self.wait_for_object(self.delete_shortcut, timeout=15). If dialog appears, clicks confirm_delete button using self.click_me(self.confirm_delete) to confirm deletion. If dialog does not appear, logs info message. Finally waits for add_shortcut_dots to appear using self.wait_for_object(self.add_shortcut_dots, timeout=10) confirming the shortcut list reloaded after deletion.

Deletes a shortcut by combining the initiation and confirmation steps. Calls self.existing_shortcut_delete() to open the delete option and click it. Calls self.shortcut_delete() to confirm the deletion in the dialog. Prints success message 'shortcut deleted successfully' to console.

Navigates to the edit screen of a shortcut (appears to be misnamed - should be navigate_to_edit_shortcut). Calls self.navigate_to_shortcuts() to open the Shortcuts page. Checks if add_shortcut_dots is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.add_shortcut_dots, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.add_shortcut_dots). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Checks if edit option is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.edit, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.edit). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. This method is identical to navigate_to_edit_shortcut() and may be a duplicate.

Opens the shortcut contextual menu and clicks the Start option to execute the shortcut. Checks if add_shortcut_dots is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.add_shortcut_dots, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.add_shortcut_dots) to open the contextual menu. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Checks if start_drop_down is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.start_drop_down, timeout=10). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.start_drop_down) to start shortcut execution. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False.

Executes a shortcut and clicks the Scan button in the scanner screen. Calls self.click_the_shortcut_start() to start the shortcut execution. Checks if scanner screen is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.scanner, self.basic_wait_time). If displayed, clicks scan_button using self.click_me(self.scan_button) and logs success. Then checks if scanning progress indicator appears using self.element_displayed(self.scanning, self.basic_wait_time). If scanning appears, logs success. If not, logs error and asserts False. If scanner screen does not appear, logs error and asserts False.

Proceeds with shortcut execution after scanning by applying edge detection and selecting printer. Checks if detect_edge_apply button is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.detect_edge_apply, timeout=30). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.detect_edge_apply) to apply edge detection. Checks if shortcut_text (Start button) is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.shortcut_text, timeout=80). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.shortcut_text) and logs success. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Checks if printer_driver_check_box is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.printer_driver_check_box, timeout=80). If displayed, logs success indicating printer options screen appeared. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address to send scanned files to

parameters.name: file_type
parameters.description: File type for scanned output (PDF or JPEG) - currently not used in method body

functions.what_it_does: Configures a shortcut to send scanned files via email only by disabling print and cloud toggles. Waits for print_email_and_save section using self.wait_for_object(self.print_email_and_save, timeout=40) and clicks it using self.click_me(). Waits for print_toggle and clicks it to disable print destination. Waits for cloud_toggle and clicks it to disable cloud destination. Waits for email_drop_down and email_to field. Clicks email_to using self.click_me(self.email_to) and types the email address using pyautogui.write(email, interval=0.1). Contains commented-out code for PDF file type selection. Waits for save_shortcut_name and clicks it to save. Waits for start_shortcut and clicks it to execute immediately. Waits 30 seconds using time.sleep(30) for shortcut to process.

Executes scan in shortcut workflow and returns to home screen after completion. Checks if scanner screen is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.scanner, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks scan_button using self.click_me(self.scan_button, timeout=60) and logs success. If not displayed, asserts False with error message. Checks if add_button appears using self.element_displayed(self.add_button, timeout=60). If displayed, clicks shortcut_text (Start button) using self.click_me(self.shortcut_text, timeout=60) and logs success. If not displayed, asserts False. Waits for shortcuts_on_its_way success message using self.wait_for_object(self.shortcuts_on_its_way, timeout=60). If appears, logs success. If not, asserts False. Checks if home button is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.home, timeout=60). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.home) and logs success. If not displayed, asserts False.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address to send scanned files to

parameters.name: file_type
parameters.description: File type for scanned output (PDF or JPEG)

functions.what_it_does: Complete end-to-end workflow for creating a scan-to-email shortcut and executing it. Calls self.navigate_to_create_your_own_shortcut() to open the shortcut wizard. Calls self.enter_email_turn_off_print_cloud_toggles(email, file_type) to configure email-only destination with the provided email address. Calls self.click_scan_and_home() to execute the scan and return to home screen. Logs completion message 'scanned files to email by creating new shortcut is completed...' using logging.info().

parameters.name: shortcut_name
parameters.description: Name to assign to the email shortcut

functions.what_it_does: Adds a shortcut with only email destination enabled. Wrapped in try-except block for error handling. Calls self.navigate_destination_add_shortcut() to reach the destination configuration screen. Clicks short_cut_name field using self.click_me(self.short_cut_name), waits 1 second using time.sleep(1), then types the shortcut_name using pyautogui.write(shortcut_name, interval=0.1). Checks if email_toggle is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.email_toggle, timeout=20). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.email_toggle), waits 2 seconds, and logs success. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False with error message. If any exception occurs, logs the error using logging.error() and re-raises the exception.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Invalid email address to test error validation

functions.what_it_does: Validates that HP Smart displays an error message when an invalid email address is entered in shortcut configuration. Wrapped in try-except block. Calls self.add_shortcut_default_email(shortcut_name=email) to create email shortcut using the invalid email as the shortcut name. In nested try-except, checks if email_to field is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.email_to, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.email_to) and types the invalid email using pyautogui.write(email, interval=0.1). Logs success. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. In another nested try-except, checks if save_shortcut_name is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.save_shortcut_name, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.save_shortcut_name) and logs success. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Constructs expected_error locator as (AppiumBy.NAME, f'Invalid emails: {email}'). Checks if error message is displayed using self.element_displayed(expected_error, timeout=20). If displayed, logs success. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. If any exception occurs at any level, logs the error and re-raises.

parameters.name: shortcut_name
parameters.description: Name to assign to the shortcut, defaults to 'test'

functions.docstring: Add a shortcut with the given name and save it.
functions.what_it_does: Adds and saves a print shortcut with the specified name. Wrapped in try-except block. Calls self.add_shortcut_default_print(shortcut_name=shortcut_name) to configure the print shortcut. Checks if save_shortcut_name is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.save_shortcut_name, timeout=15). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.save_shortcut_name). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Waits for my_shortcuts to appear using self.wait_for_object(self.my_shortcuts, timeout=30). Checks if my_shortcuts is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.my_shortcuts, timeout=10) and clicks it using self.click_me(self.my_shortcuts). Logs success message with shortcut name. If any exception occurs, logs the error and re-raises.

parameters.name: duplicate_name
parameters.description: Duplicate shortcut name to test rename warning, defaults to 'test'

functions.docstring: Try to add a shortcut with a duplicate name, handle rename warning, and click rename button.
functions.what_it_does: Tests the duplicate name warning by attempting to create a shortcut with an existing name. Wrapped in try-except block. Clicks add_shortcut using self.click_me(self.add_shortcut). Clicks create_your_own_shortcut using self.click_me(self.create_your_own_shortcut). Clicks short_cut_name field using self.click_me(self.short_cut_name), waits 1 second, then types duplicate_name using pyautogui.write(duplicate_name, interval=0.1). Checks if short_cut_name is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.short_cut_name, timeout=20). If displayed, clicks print_toggle using self.click_me(self.print_toggle). If not displayed, asserts False. Checks if save_shortcut_name is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.save_shortcut_name, timeout=15). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.save_shortcut_name). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Checks if rename_shortcut_warning is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.rename_shortcut_warning, timeout=20). If displayed, logs success and clicks rename_button using self.click_me(self.rename_button). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. If any exception occurs, logs the error and re-raises.

parameters.name: invalid_name
parameters.description: Invalid shortcut name containing special characters to test validation

functions.docstring: Enter an invalid shortcut name and validate the error message.
functions.what_it_does: Validates that HP Smart displays an error message when an invalid shortcut name with special characters is entered. Wrapped in try-except block. Clicks short_cut_name field using self.click_me(self.short_cut_name), waits 1 second, then types invalid_name using pyautogui.write(invalid_name, interval=0.1). Checks if save_shortcut_name is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.save_shortcut_name, timeout=15). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.save_shortcut_name). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Checks if short_cut_name_invalid error message is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.short_cut_name_invalid, timeout=20). If displayed, logs success. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. If any exception occurs, logs the error and re-raises.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address to configure as recipient

functions.docstring: Create a shortcut with the given email.
functions.what_it_does: Creates and saves an email-only shortcut with the specified name and email address. Wrapped in try-except block. Calls self.add_shortcut_default_email(shortcut_name=shortcut_name) to configure email shortcut. Checks if email_to field is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.email_to, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.email_to) and types the email using pyautogui.write(email, interval=0.1). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Checks if save_shortcut_name is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.save_shortcut_name, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.save_shortcut_name). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Waits for my_shortcuts to appear using self.wait_for_object(self.my_shortcuts, timeout=30). Checks if my_shortcuts is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.my_shortcuts, timeout=10). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.my_shortcuts) and logs success with shortcut name and email. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. If any exception occurs, logs the error and re-raises.

parameters.name: updated_email
parameters.description: New email address to replace the existing one

functions.docstring: Edit the shortcut and update the email, ensuring the old email is fully removed and new email is entered.
functions.what_it_does: Edits an existing shortcut to update the email address by fully replacing the old email. Wrapped in try-except block. Checks if add_shortcut is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.add_shortcut, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks add_shortcut_dots using self.click_me(self.add_shortcut_dots) and logs success. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Checks if edit option is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.edit, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.edit) and logs success. If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Checks if email_to field is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.email_to, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.email_to), logs clearing message, performs pyautogui.doubleClick() to select all text, waits 0.5 seconds, then types updated_email using pyautogui.write(updated_email, interval=0.5). If not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Checks if edit_delete_button is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.edit_delete_button, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.edit_delete_button) and logs success. Then checks if yes_delete confirmation is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.yes_delete, timeout=40). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.yes_delete), logs confirmation, and waits for add_shortcut using self.wait_for_object(self.add_shortcut, timeout=30). If yes_delete not displayed, logs error and asserts False. If edit_delete_button not displayed, logs error and asserts False. Logs final success message with updated email. If any exception occurs, logs the error and re-raises.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Timeout in seconds to wait for the popup to appear

functions.what_it_does: Handles the Windows 'Remember sign-in info?' popup that appears after cloud provider authentication by clicking Skip. Waits for remember_sign_in_popup to appear using self.wait_for_object(self.remember_sign_in_popup, timeout=timeout). If popup appears, checks if remember_sign_in_skip button is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.remember_sign_in_skip, timeout=timeout). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.remember_sign_in_skip), logs success, and returns True. If popup does not appear or skip button not found, returns False.

functions.method_signature: def create_shortcut_save_to_cloud(self, google_email=None, google_password=None, onedrive_email=None, onedrive_password=None, dropbox_email=None, dropbox_password=None, shortcut_name='Save to Cloud Shortcut')

parameters.name: google_email
parameters.description: Google account email for Google Drive authentication

parameters.name: google_password
parameters.description: Google account password for Google Drive authentication

parameters.name: onedrive_email
parameters.description: Microsoft account email for OneDrive authentication

parameters.name: onedrive_password
parameters.description: Microsoft account password for OneDrive authentication

parameters.name: dropbox_email
parameters.description: Dropbox account email for Dropbox authentication

parameters.name: dropbox_password
parameters.description: Dropbox account password for Dropbox authentication

parameters.name: shortcut_name
parameters.description: Name for the cloud storage shortcut

functions.docstring: Create shortcut and optionally sign into cloud providers.
functions.what_it_does: Creates a shortcut with cloud storage destination and optionally authenticates with Google Drive, OneDrive, or Dropbox. Wrapped in try-except block. Calls self.add_shortcut_default_print(shortcut_name=shortcut_name) to start shortcut creation. Checks if save_files_cloud_toggle is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.save_files_cloud_toggle, timeout=20). If not displayed, logs error and returns False. Clicks the toggle using self.click_me(self.save_files_cloud_toggle) and logs success. Detects available cloud providers by checking if google_drive_sign_in, onedrive_sign_in, and dropbox_sign_in are displayed using self.element_displayed(locator, timeout=5) for each. Stores found providers in providers_found list. If no providers found, logs error and returns False. For each found provider, checks if corresponding credentials are supplied. If credentials provided, clicks the sign-in button using self.click_me(locator) and waits 5 seconds. For Google Drive: waits for google_sign_in_page using self.wait_for_object(self.google_sign_in_page, 20). Waits for google_email_field, finds element using self.driver.find_element(*self.google_email_field), clears it, and sends google_email using send_keys(). Clicks google_next_button, waits 3 seconds. Waits for google_password_field, finds element, clears it, and sends google_password. Clicks google_next_button, waits 3 seconds. If google_allow_button appears using self.wait_for_object(self.google_allow_button, 15), clicks it and logs success. Calls self.handle_remember_sign_in_popup(timeout=5). For OneDrive: waits for microsoft_email_field (not defined in locators - appears to be missing), finds element, clears it, sends onedrive_email. Clicks microsoft_next_btn, waits 2 seconds. Finds microsoft_password_field, clears it, sends onedrive_password. Clicks microsoft_signin_btn. Calls self.handle_remember_sign_in_popup(timeout=5). For Dropbox: waits for dropbox_email_field (not defined in locators), finds element, clears it, sends dropbox_email. Finds dropbox_password_field, clears it, sends dropbox_password. Clicks dropbox_login_btn. Waits 2 seconds after each provider. If any exception occurs during cloud setup, logs error and returns False. After all providers processed, checks if save_shortcut_name is displayed using self.element_displayed(self.save_shortcut_name, timeout=20). If displayed, clicks it using self.click_me(self.save_shortcut_name), logs success, and returns True. If not displayed, logs error and returns False.

parameters.name: combo_locator
parameters.description: Appium locator tuple for the combo box to validate

parameters.name: expected_options
parameters.description: List of expected option text values that should be present in the dropdown

functions.docstring:
Validate dropdown contains expected options

combo_locator : locator of combo button
expected_options : list of visible option texts
functions.what_it_does: Validates that a combo box dropdown contains all expected options. Asserts that combo_locator is displayed using self.element_displayed(combo_locator, timeout=10) with error message 'Combo box not displayed'. Clicks the combo box using self.click_me(combo_locator) to open the dropdown and logs 'Combo opened'. Initializes empty missing list. For each text in expected_options, constructs an option locator as (AppiumBy.XPATH, f"//ListItem[@Name='{text}']"). Checks if option is displayed using self.element_displayed(option, timeout=5). If displayed, logs success with option text. If not displayed, logs error and appends text to missing list. After checking all options, clicks save_shortcut_name using self.click_me(self.save_shortcut_name) to close the dropdown. Asserts that missing list is empty with error message showing missing options if any were not found.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: Shortcuts is a page object class for HP Smart Windows app automation using Appium WinAppDriver. It inherits from HPSmartBasicActions and is ALWAYS instantiated with an active Appium driver from the appium_driver pytest fixture (NOT driver=None). All locators are class-level Appium tuples using AppiumBy strategies (NAME, ACCESSIBILITY_ID, XPATH, ID). The class provides comprehensive shortcut lifecycle management including creation with multiple destination types (print, email, cloud), editing, deletion, execution with scan jobs, and validation. The standard workflow for creating a shortcut is: navigate_to_shortcuts() → delete_existing_shortcut() → navigate_to_create_your_own_shortcut() → click create_your_own_shortcut → configure name and toggles → add_shortcut_email() or enable cloud → save_shortcut_name → verify in my_shortcuts. For executing shortcuts: click_the_shortcut_start() → click_scan() → shortcut_print_job_through_scan() → validate completion. The class includes extensive error handling with try-except blocks and assertion-based validation. Cloud provider authentication is complex and handles Google Drive, OneDrive, and Dropbox sign-in flows with credential input and permission granting. The class uses pyautogui.write() for text input instead of Appium send_keys() in many places. Timeout constants are defined at class level for reuse across methods.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Driver module class for HP Smart print driver dialog automation on Windows. Contains SmartDriver which inherits HPSmartBasicActions and provides Appium tuple locators plus uiautomation-based methods for opening documents from data/ folder, triggering Windows print dialog via Ctrl+P keyboard shortcut, selecting printers by name, navigating driver settings tabs, configuring print options, submitting print jobs, and validating HP Smart toast notifications for print job completion.

imports.import_statement: from selenium.webdriver.support.ui import WebDriverWait as wd
from selenium.webdriver.support import expected_conditions as EC
from appium import webdriver as appium_webdriver

classes.class_purpose: Driver module class for automating HP Smart print driver dialogs on Windows using a hybrid approach of Appium tuple locators combined with uiautomation, pywinauto, and pynput for actual interactions. Inherits from HPSmartBasicActions. Defines class-level Appium tuple locators for all HP Smart driver dialog controls but uses uiautomation and pywinauto for most interactions instead of Appium driver methods. Provides complete print workflow: open document → trigger print dialog via Ctrl+P → select printer → open driver properties → configure settings across tabs → submit print job → validate toast notifications.

locators.ui_element_description: Basic tab button in HP Smart print driver properties dialog identified by accessibility ID, used to navigate to Basic settings tab containing pages per sheet, print quality, color mode, and page border options.
Pages per Sheet dropdown control in HP Smart driver Basic tab for selecting how many document pages print on each physical sheet.
Print Quality control in HP Smart driver Basic tab for selecting output quality level (Draft, Normal, Best).
Color mode control in HP Smart driver dialog identified by AutomationId XPath, used to switch between color and black-and-white printing modes.
Page Border toggle control in HP Smart driver Basic tab for enabling/disabling borders around each page when using multiple pages per sheet.
Paper Size dropdown list in HP Smart driver Document tab for selecting paper dimensions (Letter, A4, Legal).
Document tab button in HP Smart driver properties dialog that navigates to document-specific settings including paper size, collation, rotation, and orientation.
Collate toggle control in HP Smart driver Document tab for enabling collation of multi-page print jobs.
Rotate 180 degrees toggle control in HP Smart driver Document tab for rotating printed output.
Landscape orientation option in HP Smart driver Document tab for selecting landscape page orientation.
Ready to print status text in HP Smart driver dialog indicating the printer is ready and print job can be submitted.
Print button in HP Smart driver properties dialog used to confirm settings and close the dialog (note: locator name is misleading - it's the Print button not OK button).
Main Print button in HP Smart app or driver confirmation screen that submits the final print job after all settings configured.
Advanced tab button in HP Smart driver properties dialog for accessing advanced print settings such as copy count, duplex printing, and other advanced options.

functions.method_signature: def __init__(self, driver: object, timeout: object = 10) -> None

parameters.name: driver
parameters.description: Appium WebDriver instance or None - typically None for SmartDriver since it uses uiautomation/pywinauto internally

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Default timeout in seconds for element wait operations, defaults to 10

functions.what_it_does: Initializes SmartDriver by calling parent HPSmartBasicActions constructor with super().__init__(driver), storing driver reference as self.driver and timeout value as self.timeout. All locators are defined as class-level attributes so no locator initialization happens in __init__. This constructor supports being called with driver=None for tests that use uiautomation/pywinauto internally instead of active Appium session.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: SmartDriver is a hybrid driver module that defines Appium tuple locators but primarily uses uiautomation, pywinauto, pynput, and pyautogui for actual Windows UI automation instead of Appium WinAppDriver. It is ALWAYS instantiated with driver=None. The class provides extensive print driver automation including document opening via subprocess, print dialog triggering via keyboard shortcuts, printer selection via uiautomation, driver properties configuration via uiautomation combo boxes and toggles, and print job validation via toast notification detection. The standard workflow is: open_document() → trigger_print_dialog() → select_printer() → print_using_system_dialog() → select_printer_using_system_dialog() → click_more_settings() → select_settings_tab() → [configure settings] → click_ok_button() → click_print() → click_print_button() → validate_hp_smart_print_toast_notifications(). The class includes sophisticated methods for handling Windows print dialogs, Microsoft Office print dialogs, and HP Smart driver-specific dialogs. Many methods use uiautomation WindowControl, ButtonControl, ComboBoxControl, and TextControl for UI interaction. The validate_hp_smart_print_toast_notifications() method is the primary verification method returning boolean for test assertions. The class demonstrates advanced Windows automation patterns including window attachment, control traversal, toggle state management, and toast notification detection.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Driver module class for HP Smart Universal Printing Driver (SUPD) automation using uiautomation library. Contains SUPDDriver which inherits HPSmartBasicActions and provides methods for interacting with SUPD driver dialogs including tab navigation, print quality selection, OK button clicking, copy count increment, collate toggle, preset management (save, select, revert), and color mode selection in Microsoft Edge print dialog.

classes.class_purpose: Driver module class for automating HP Smart Universal Printing Driver (SUPD) v5.02.1 dialogs using uiautomation library. Inherits from HPSmartBasicActions. Does not define class-level locators - all element identification is done inline using uiautomation AutomationId, Name, and ClassName matching. Provides methods for navigating driver tabs, selecting print quality, managing copy count, toggling collate, saving and selecting presets, reverting to factory defaults, and selecting color modes in browser print dialogs.

parameters.name: driver
parameters.description: Appium WebDriver instance or None - typically None for SUPDDriver since it uses uiautomation internally

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Default timeout in seconds for element wait operations

functions.what_it_does: Initializes SUPDDriver by calling parent HPSmartBasicActions constructor with super().__init__(driver), storing driver reference as self.driver and timeout value as self.timeout. No locators are defined as class-level attributes - all element identification is done inline in methods using uiautomation.

parameters.name: tab_name
parameters.description: Name of the settings tab to select (e.g., 'Basic', 'Document', 'Advanced')

functions.docstring: To select the settings tab in the HP Universal Printing Driver application.
functions.what_it_does: Selects a settings tab in the HP Smart Universal Printing Driver dialog by tab name. Uses uiautomation to find a RadioButtonControl with Name matching tab_name parameter using auto.RadioButtonControl(Name=tab_name). Double-clicks the tab using tab.DoubleClick() to activate it. Logs the action using logging.info() with formatted message showing which tab was clicked.

parameters.name: quality
parameters.description: Desired print quality option to select (e.g., 'Best', 'Normal', 'Draft')

functions.docstring:
Selecting Printing Quality from the dropdown in the HP Universal Printing Driver application.
:param quality: The desired print quality option to select (e.g., "Best", "Normal", "Draft").
:return: None
functions.what_it_does: Selects print quality from the Print Quality dropdown in SUPD driver dialog. Uses uiautomation to find ComboBoxControl with AutomationId 'Part.JobUserResolution' and Name 'Print Quality' using auto.ComboBoxControl(AutomationId='Part.JobUserResolution', Name='Print Quality'). Expands the dropdown using combo.GetExpandCollapsePattern().Expand() and waits 1 second. Finds the desired quality option as ListItemControl with Name matching quality parameter using auto.ListItemControl(Name=quality). Checks if the control is a ListItemControl and has SelectionItemPattern. If yes, selects it using option.GetSelectionItemPattern().Select(). If not, clicks it using option.Click().

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Timeout in seconds to wait for OK button to exist

functions.docstring:
Locate and click the 'OK' button in the HP Universal Print window.
Handles case when the button is off-screen due to resolution.
functions.what_it_does: Locates and clicks the OK button in the HP Smart Universal Printing window, handling cases where button may be off-screen. Wrapped in try-except block. Uses uiautomation to find ButtonControl with AutomationId 'App.OK' and Name 'OK' using auto.ButtonControl(AutomationId='App.OK', Name='OK'). Checks if button exists within timeout using ok_btn.Exists(timeout, 1). If not found, raises LookupError. Attempts to bring button into view by calling ok_btn.SetFocus() and waits 1 second. Clicks the button using ok_btn.Click(). Logs success using logging.info() and returns True. If any exception occurs, logs warning with exception details using logging.exception().

functions.docstring: Closes the current window using Ctrl+W
functions.what_it_does: Closes the current active window by sending Ctrl+W keyboard shortcut. Wrapped in try-except block. Creates pynput keyboard Controller instance. Presses Ctrl key using keyboard.press(Key.ctrl), presses 'w' key using keyboard.press('w'), releases 'w' using keyboard.release('w'), then releases Ctrl using keyboard.release(Key.ctrl). Logs success using logging.info(). Waits 45 seconds using time.sleep(45) after closing window. If any exception occurs, logs error using logging.error() and re-raises the exception.

parameters.name: user_input
parameters.description: Number of times to increment the copy count

functions.docstring:
Increase the copy count using the Next sibling of the Copy Count TextBox.

:param user_input: Number of times to increment
functions.what_it_does: Increases the copy count in SUPD driver by clicking the plus button multiple times. Uses uiautomation to locate the Copy Count TextBox using EditControl with AutomationId 'Part.JobCopiesAllDocuments.TextBox' and Name 'TextBox' using auto.EditControl(AutomationId='Part.JobCopiesAllDocuments.TextBox', Name='TextBox'). Checks if control exists within 10 seconds using copy_count_box.Exists(10). If not found, raises LookupError. Gets the plus button as the next sibling control using copy_count_box.GetNextSiblingControl(). Retrieves initial copy count value using copy_count_box.GetValuePattern().Value and converts to int. Logs initial value. Loops user_input times, clicking plus_button using plus_button.Click() and waiting 0.2 seconds after each click using time.sleep(0.2) to allow UI refresh. Retrieves final value and logs it. Calculates expected value as initial_value + user_input. Compares final value to expected value. If match, logs success. If mismatch, logs error with both values.

parameters.name: ctrl
parameters.description: uiautomation Control object to get toggle state from

functions.docstring:
Returns the toggle state:
    0 = Off, 1 = On, 2 = Indeterminate
functions.what_it_does: Helper method to retrieve the toggle state of a control. Wrapped in try-except block. Waits 0.5 seconds using time.sleep(0.5) to give UI time to settle. Gets TogglePattern from control using ctrl.GetTogglePattern(). If pattern exists, returns tp.ToggleState which is an integer (0=Off, 1=On, 2=Indeterminate). If any exception occurs, logs warning using logging.warning() and returns 0 as fallback.

functions.docstring:
Ensures the Collate toggle is in the desired state.
desired_state: "On" or "Off"
functions.what_it_does: Ensures the Collate toggle in SUPD driver is in the desired state (On or Off). First focuses the main print window by finding WindowControl with Name 'HP Smart Universal Printing (v5.02.1)' using auto.WindowControl(Name='HP Smart Universal Printing (v5.02.1)'), calling main_window.SetFocus(), and waiting 0.5 seconds. Finds the window using WindowControl with ClassName 'Window' using auto.WindowControl(ClassName='Window'). Finds the Collate toggle using ButtonControl with AutomationId 'Part.DocumentCollate' using window.ButtonControl(AutomationId='Part.DocumentCollate'). Checks if toggle exists within 5 seconds using toggle.Exists(5, 1). If not found, raises Exception. Converts desired_state to integer (1 for 'on', 0 for 'off') using desired_val = 1 if desired_state.lower() == 'on' else 0. Gets current toggle state using self._get_toggle_state(toggle). If current state matches desired state, logs info message and returns. If states don't match, toggles the control using toggle.GetTogglePattern().Toggle() and waits 0.5 seconds. Gets new state using self._get_toggle_state(toggle). If new state matches desired state, logs success. If not, raises RuntimeError.

parameters.name: name_preset
parameters.description: Name of the preset to select from the preset dropdown

functions.what_it_does: Selects a preset by name from the Preset ComboBox in SUPD driver. Logs search message. Finds preset ComboBox using auto.ComboBoxControl(AutomationId='ui_PresetComboBox'). If exists within 2 seconds, logs success and expands dropdown using preset_combo.GetExpandCollapsePattern().Expand(waitTime=2), waits 0.5 seconds. Scrolls up to top of list by finding PageUp button using auto.ButtonControl(AutomationId='PageUp', ClassName='RepeatButton') and clicking it 5 times with 0.1 second delay between clicks. Sets focus on preset combo using preset_combo.SetFocus(). Loops up to 30 times searching for the preset item. In each iteration, finds ListItemControl with Name matching name_preset using auto.ListItemControl(Name=name_preset). If item exists within 1 second, clicks it using labels_item.Click(), logs success, and returns. If not found, sends DOWN arrow key using preset_combo.SendKeys('{DOWN}') to scroll down one item and waits 0.1 seconds. If item not found after 30 iterations, logs warning. If ComboBox not found initially, logs warning.

Clicks the Preset Save button in SUPD driver to save current settings as a preset. Wrapped in try-except block. Attempts to find ButtonControl with Name 'PresetSaveButton' using auto.ButtonControl(Name='PresetSaveButton'). If button exists within 3 seconds, double-clicks it using button.DoubleClick(), logs success, and returns True. If button not found by Name, logs error and attempts fallback approach. Finds ImageControl with AutomationId 'iconImage' using auto.ImageControl(AutomationId='iconImage'). If icon exists within 3 seconds, gets its parent control using icon.GetParentControl(). Checks if parent is a ButtonControl by checking parent_button.ControlTypeName == 'ButtonControl'. If yes, double-clicks parent using parent_button.DoubleClick(), logs success, and returns True. If fallback also fails, raises RuntimeError. If any exception occurs, logs exception details using logging.exception() and re-raises.

parameters.name: preset_name
parameters.description: Name to enter for the new preset

functions.what_it_does: Enters a preset name in the preset save dialog TextBox. Finds the HP Smart Universal Printing window using auto.WindowControl(Name='HP Smart Universal Printing (v5.02.1)'). If window not found within 5 seconds, logs error and returns. Finds the PresetPopupPage custom control using smart_win.CustomControl(Name='PresetPopupPage'). If popup not found within 5 seconds, logs error and returns. Finds the TextBox edit control inside popup using popup.EditControl(ClassName='TextBox'). If TextBox not found within 5 seconds, logs error and returns. Wrapped in try-except block. Sets focus on edit box using edit_box.SetFocus() and waits 0.2 seconds. Sends keys to enter preset_name using edit_box.SendKeys(preset_name, interval=0.08) with 0.08 second interval between characters. Logs success. If any exception occurs, logs error.

parameters.name: preset_name
parameters.description: Preset name parameter (not used in method body)

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the Save button in the preset save dialog. Finds TextControl with Name 'Save' using auto.TextControl(Name='Save'). If control exists within 5 seconds, logs success and clicks it using save_text.Click(). If not found, logs warning.

Reverts SUPD driver settings to factory defaults by clicking Revert button and confirming with Restore. Waits 10 seconds using time.sleep(10). Wrapped in try-except block. Finds PresetRevertButton using auto.ButtonControl(Name='PresetRevertButton'). If button exists within 3 seconds and is enabled, double-clicks it using button.DoubleClick() and logs success. Then finds Restore button using auto.ButtonControl(AutomationId='ui_FeaturePopupIDS_RESTOREButton'). If restore button exists within 3 seconds and is enabled, clicks it using restore_btn.Click() and logs success. If restore button not found or not enabled, logs warning. Returns True if revert button was clicked. If revert button not enabled or not found, logs info message indicating passing through. If any exception occurs, logs exception details using logging.exception() and re-raises.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Timeout in seconds to wait for print window

functions.docstring:
Selects a color mode (Color, Monochrome, Grayscale) from the print dialog list.
Tries ComboBoxControl first, then falls back to TextControl/ListItemControl for robustness.
functions.what_it_does: Selects color mode from Microsoft Edge print dialog with multiple fallback strategies. Logs attempt message. Finds print window using auto.WindowControl(Name='Microsoft Edge - Print'). If window not found within timeout, logs error and raises Exception. Attempts primary approach: finds ComboBoxControl with Name 'Color mode' using print_win.ComboBoxControl(Name='Color mode'). If combo exists within 5 seconds, logs success and clicks it to expand. If combo exists but not detected properly, clicks anyway, waits 0.6 seconds, then finds ListItemControl with Name matching color_mode using print_win.ListItemControl(Name=color_mode). If option exists within 5 seconds, double-clicks it using option.DoubleClick(), logs success, and returns. If option not found, logs warning and continues to fallback. If ComboBox not found, logs info about trying fallback. Fallback 1: finds TextControl with Name matching color_mode using auto.TextControl(Name=color_mode). If exists within 5 seconds, double-clicks it, logs success, and returns. Fallback 2: finds ListItemControl with Name matching color_mode using auto.ListItemControl(Name=color_mode). If exists within 5 seconds, double-clicks it, logs success, and returns. If all approaches fail, logs error. Wrapped in try-except block that attempts to enumerate all ListItemControls in print window for debugging using print_win.ListItemControl().GetChildren() and logs each item name. If enumeration fails, logs warning. Finally raises Exception if color mode not found.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: SUPDDriver is a driver module class for HP Smart Universal Printing Driver (SUPD) v5.02.1 automation using uiautomation library exclusively. It inherits from HPSmartBasicActions and is ALWAYS instantiated with driver=None. Unlike other driver classes in this framework, SUPDDriver does NOT define any class-level locators - all element identification is done inline in methods using uiautomation AutomationId, Name, and ClassName matching. The class provides methods for common SUPD driver operations including tab navigation, print quality selection, copy count management, collate toggle, and preset management (save, select, revert to factory defaults). The select_color_mode_from_list() method is specifically designed for Microsoft Edge print dialog interaction with multiple fallback strategies for robustness. The class demonstrates advanced uiautomation patterns including ExpandCollapsePattern for dropdowns, TogglePattern for toggles, SelectionItemPattern for list items, and GetNextSiblingControl() for finding related controls. The preset management methods show complex UI traversal including scrolling with PageUp button, keyboard navigation with SendKeys, and parent control access. The class uses extensive error handling with try-except blocks and detailed logging at every step.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Driver module class for HP Universal Fax Driver (UFD) automation using uiautomation library. Contains UfdDriver which inherits HPSmartBasicActions and provides methods for interacting with HP Fax Finder window, selecting printers by IP address or network search, configuring fax settings (resolution, color mode, contrast), entering fax numbers, adding cover pages, sending faxes, and validating fax job status in the Fax Status window.

classes.class_purpose: Driver module class for automating HP Universal Fax Driver (UFD) dialogs and HP Fax Finder window using uiautomation library. Inherits from HPSmartBasicActions. Does not define class-level locators - all element identification is done inline using uiautomation WindowControl, TreeControl, ButtonControl, EditControl, ComboBoxControl, RadioButtonControl, SliderControl, and ListControl with Name, AutomationId, and RegexName matching. Provides complete fax workflow: find printer by IP or network → select printer → configure fax settings → enter fax numbers → add cover page → send fax → validate fax status.

parameters.name: driver
parameters.description: Appium WebDriver instance or None - typically None for UfdDriver since it uses uiautomation internally

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Default timeout in seconds for element wait operations

functions.what_it_does: Initializes UfdDriver by calling parent HPSmartBasicActions constructor with super().__init__(driver), storing driver reference as self.driver, timeout value as self.timeout, and main window name as self.main_win_name = 'HP Fax Finder'. No locators are defined as class-level attributes - all element identification is done inline in methods using uiautomation.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: UfdDriver is a driver module class for HP Universal Fax Driver automation using uiautomation library exclusively. It inherits from HPSmartBasicActions and is ALWAYS instantiated with driver=None. The class does NOT define any class-level locators - all element identification is done inline in methods. The class provides comprehensive fax automation including printer discovery via IP address entry or network search, fax settings configuration (resolution, color mode, contrast slider), fax number entry with add/remove operations, cover page selection with checkboxes, fax sending, and fax status validation with retry logic. The get_window_based_title() helper method is used throughout to find windows by partial title with retry logic. The class demonstrates advanced uiautomation patterns including TreeControl navigation for printer selection, SliderControl with RangeValuePattern for contrast adjustment, ListControl with row/column parsing for fax status validation, and RegexName matching for flexible window title matching. The validate_fax_status() method shows sophisticated retry logic with configurable attempts and delays. The class uses extensive error handling with try-except blocks and detailed logging. The select_printer_network() method includes complex logic for scrolling items into view and waiting for controls to become enabled. The set_contrast_slider() method includes step-to-raw value mapping for precise slider positioning. The class is specifically designed for HP LaserJet Pro MFP printers with fax capability.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Driver module class for HP Universal Printing Driver (UPD) automation using uiautomation library. Contains UpdDriver which inherits HPSmartBasicActions and provides methods for interacting with UPD Printing Preferences window including tab navigation, dropdown selection, checkbox and radio button toggling, copy count verification, preset management (save, select), tree view navigation, and print job status verification via Windows print queue.

classes.class_purpose: Driver module class for automating HP Universal Printing Driver (UPD) Printing Preferences window using uiautomation library. Inherits from HPSmartBasicActions. Does not define class-level locators - all element identification is done inline using uiautomation WindowControl, TabItemControl, ComboBoxControl, CheckBoxControl, RadioButtonControl, ButtonControl, EditControl, TreeItemControl, and ListItemControl with Name, AutomationId, and ClassName matching. Provides methods for configuring all UPD settings, managing presets, and verifying print job status via Windows system tray and print queue.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: UpdDriver is a driver module class for HP Universal Printing Driver automation using uiautomation library exclusively. It inherits from HPSmartBasicActions and is ALWAYS instantiated with driver=None. The class does NOT define any class-level locators - all element identification is done inline in methods. The class provides comprehensive UPD driver automation including window retrieval with get_printing_preferences_window(), tab navigation with click_upd_driver_tabs(), dropdown selection with select_dropdown_option() using next sibling pattern, checkbox toggling with enable_disable_checkbox() using TogglePattern, radio button selection with enable_disable_radio_button() using SelectionItemPattern, copy count management with click_increase_button() and click_decrease_button(), preset management with click_save_as() and select_shortcut(), tree view navigation with select_value_under_tree_view(), and print job status verification with verify_print_job_status() and wait_for_printed_status() that interact with Windows system tray and print queue. The verify_print_job_status() method demonstrates advanced Windows automation by accessing the system tray overflow window, right-clicking printer icon, and opening print queue. The wait_for_printed_status() method shows sophisticated print job monitoring with timeout-based polling and job name matching. The class uses extensive error handling with try-except blocks and detailed logging. The class imports JOB_NAME constant from config.constants for print job identification. The enable_disable_checkbox() and enable_disable_radio_button() methods include comprehensive state validation with initial state checking and post-action verification. The class is designed for HP Universal Printing Driver which has a different UI structure than HP Smart driver.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Driver module class for HP Universal Printing Driver (UPD) automation using uiautomation library. Contains UpdDriver which inherits HPSmartBasicActions and provides methods for interacting with UPD Printing Preferences window including tab navigation, dropdown selection, checkbox and radio button toggling, copy count verification, preset management (save, select), tree view navigation, and print job status verification via Windows print queue.

classes.class_purpose: Driver module class for automating HP Universal Printing Driver (UPD) Printing Preferences window using uiautomation library. Inherits from HPSmartBasicActions. Does not define class-level locators - all element identification is done inline using uiautomation WindowControl, TabItemControl, ComboBoxControl, CheckBoxControl, RadioButtonControl, ButtonControl, EditControl, TreeItemControl, and ListItemControl with Name, AutomationId, and ClassName matching. Provides methods for configuring all UPD settings, managing presets, and verifying print job status via Windows system tray and print queue.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: UpdDriver is a driver module class for HP Universal Printing Driver automation using uiautomation library exclusively. It inherits from HPSmartBasicActions and is ALWAYS instantiated with driver=None. The class does NOT define any class-level locators - all element identification is done inline in methods. Due to file size constraints, the detailed method analysis for UpdDriver was not completed in this batch report but the file structure, imports, class definition, and purpose have been fully documented. The class provides comprehensive UPD driver automation including window retrieval, tab navigation, dropdown selection, checkbox/radio button toggling, copy count management, preset management, tree view navigation, and print job status verification via Windows system tray and print queue. The class demonstrates advanced Windows automation patterns similar to SUPDDriver and UfdDriver.
